macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stock Markets Plunge into Correction Amid Middle East Tensions
items.summary: Major US indices suffered sharp declines on March 27, 2026: Dow Jones fell 793 points (1.73%) to 45,166.64 entering correction territory (down over 10% from February peak), S&P 500 dropped 1.67-1.74% to around 6,368-6,477, and Nasdaq slid 2.38%. Rising oil prices over $100/barrel due to Iran conflict fueled fears of broader sell-off and inflation pressures.

items.title: Dow Confirms Correction as Stocks Tumble on Geopolitical Risks
items.summary: Dow Jones dropped 793.47 points (1.73%) to 45,166.64, S&P 500 lost 108.31 points (1.67%) to 6,368.85, and Nasdaq declined amid escalating Middle East tensions dragging markets lower. This marks one of the weakest recent stretches with indices nearing or in correction.

items.title: Wall Street Meltdown: Markets Hit Lowest Since August on Iran War
items.summary: Dow plunged nearly 800 points on March 27, pushing it into correction; Brent crude surged over $100/barrel highest since Iran conflict began, pressuring stocks to multi-month lows. Broader indices like S&P and Nasdaq also sharply down despite year-to-date gains under current administration.

items.title: Major US Indices Enter Correction Phase with Rising Oil and Inflation Fears
items.summary: End of March sees US stocks in correction amid volatility, reassessment of inflation and rate cut expectations; oil/gas price surges intensify bond yields and global risk. Investors eye sectoral rotations favoring energy and commodities over high-valuation tech.

items.title: Panic on Wall Street: VIX Surge Signals More Selling Ahead
items.summary: Last week's breakdowns in major indices and sectors, with Dow down ~1%, S&P 500 over 2%, Nasdaq 100 in correction; VIX above 20 indicates further downside, especially in Mag 7 stocks. S&P 500 nears 10% correction threshold.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Solana Drop 6-8% Amid Options Expiry and ETF Outflows
items.summary: Major cryptocurrencies fell 6-8% this week following a $14.16 billion Bitcoin options expiry on March 27 that liquidated over 122,000 traders with $451 million in losses. Coordinated ETF outflows across BTC, ETH, and SOL marked the first simultaneous negative session for all three spot ETFs in 2026.

items.title: Bitcoin Holds $66K Support Level as Market Enters Reconfiguration Phase
items.summary: Bitcoin remains range-bound near $66,000 after 50 days, with neutral RSI momentum awaiting a catalyst. The $66,000 support level is critical, as a break below could trigger a move toward $50,000, though stablecoin supply near record $316 billion suggests capital is ready to return.

items.title: Regulation Emerges as Standalone Market Factor in March 2026
items.summary: Regulatory clarity on market structure, stablecoins, and regulatory distribution has become a primary driver of cryptocurrency market dynamics, with delays restricting institutional capital inflows into the sector.

items.title: RAIN Token Surges to #29 Market Cap Position with $3.79 Billion Valuation
items.summary: RAIN token has climbed to the 29th position by market capitalization, reaching $3.79 billion as of March 28, 2026, with on-chain data revealing sustained accumulation patterns and institutional participation levels rarely seen outside the top 20 cryptocurrencies.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: US District Judge Blocks Government Ban on Anthropic AI
items.summary: A US District Judge has temporarily blocked a government ban on Anthropic AI, along with its supply-chain risk designation. This decision impacts ongoing regulatory efforts around AI companies.

items.title: Google Unveils 'Agent Smith' Internal AI Tool for Engineering Operations
items.summary: Google has introduced 'Agent Smith', an internal AI tool that assists engineers with coding and enables 'Work From Phone' capabilities. It supports operational efficiency within the company.

items.title: Huawei Launches Atlas 350 AI Accelerator to Rival Nvidia H20
items.summary: Huawei has released the Atlas 350 AI accelerator, positioned as a competitor to Nvidia's H20 in the AI hardware market. This move intensifies competition in AI chip development.

items.title: Trump Appoints David Sacks as Co-Chair of President's Tech Advisory Council
items.summary: President Trump has named David Sacks as co-chair of the President's tech advisory council, retaining focus on AI and crypto policies. This signals continued emphasis on technology leadership.

items.title: Machine Learning Breakthrough Stabilizes High-Power Lasers at Berkeley Lab
items.summary: Researchers at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory used machine learning to stabilize high-power lasers, marking a significant advancement in laser technology.

items.title: AI Model Improves Inverse Protein Folding for Drug Design
items.summary: Researchers from the University of Sheffield and AstraZeneca developed an AI method using machine learning to enhance accuracy in inverse protein folding, aiding drug design efforts.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Riot Games Developing Open-World RPG in League of Legends Universe
items.summary: Riot Games is reportedly working on an open-world RPG project set within the League of Legends universe, according to gaming industry updates from March 28.

items.title: PUBG Battlegrounds Outlines 2026 Anti-Cheat Plans
items.summary: PUBG Battlegrounds has announced its anti-cheat strategy for 2026 as part of ongoing efforts to combat cheating in the competitive shooter.

items.title: Crimson Desert Reaches 3 Million Sales Milestone
items.summary: Pearl Abyss' Crimson Desert has achieved 3 million sales, leading to a stock rebound for the publisher.

items.title: Shadowverse Shutting Down After Ten Years
items.summary: The digital card game Shadowverse is preparing to shut down after a decade of operation.

items.title: Chained Echoes Developer Sues Publisher Over Kickstarter Fulfillment
items.summary: Mafias Linda, developer of Chained Echoes, has severed ties with publisher First Press Games and is preparing a lawsuit over the publisher's failure to send physical copies to Kickstarter backers.

items.title: Netti's Games Cuts Funding to Neagoshi Studios
items.summary: Publisher Netti's Games will cut off funding to Neagoshi Studios by May, following previous studio shutdowns including Ogre Studios.

items.title: Epic Games Store Free Game Deadline: March 29
items.summary: Epic Games Store users have until March 29 to claim a minimalist soccer game as part of the platform's weekly free game promotion.

items.title: Game Releases March 30-31: Truck Mechanic and More
items.summary: Truck Mechanic: Dangerous Parts launches on Steam March 30, with additional titles releasing through March 31.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
